understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to navigate the player character to the goal gem tile marked '+' in each level. Movement is primarily horizontal, and gravity pulls the character in a fixed direction (up or down) after each horizontal move until landing on a solid tile. The level required strategic use of movement and clicking to remove breakable blocks to create fall paths toward the goal.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Horizontal moves (MOVE_LEFT/MOVE_RIGHT) shift the player one column, then gravity automatically drops them until landing on a solid surface. CLICK removes breakable 'x' tiles to open new fall paths. The structured state's left_landing/right_landing previews showed where you'd end up after gravity resolved. Spikes (u/v) end the run on contact. UNDO reverts the last action. corridor_scan helped identify 'passage' columns where falling would take you to a new y-level. In this run, I successfully navigated through multiple sections by clicking out breakable blocks to open vertical passages, though the attempt ended at turn 16 - likely from landing on a spike or running out of turns before reaching the goal.
